dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes gradual price diffusion enabling momentum exploitation. Entry requires price above/below 5-period SMA with sufficient ATR (minimum 1% price movement). Exit on SMA crossover representing trend reversal. Context-dependent direction allows both long and short entries. 'Minimum 5 bars in trend' is implicit in SMA establishment; explicit bar-counting not directly supported by indicator set. ATR threshold (0.01 decimal = ~1%) approximates the '1.0 ATR' magnitude requirement.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal emphasizes gradual price diffusion enabling momentum exploitation. Entry requires price above/below 5-period SMA with sufficient ATR (minimum 1% price movement). Exit on SMA crossover representing trend reversal. Context-dependent direction allows both long and short entries. 'Minimum 5 bars in trend' is implicit in SMA establishment; explicit bar-counting not directly supported by indicator set. ATR threshold (0.01 decimal = ~1%) approximates the '1.0 ATR' magnitude requirement.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter trend-following positions when prices are changing gradually enough to establish momentum, as this gradual diffusion of information creates predictable price trends that momentum traders can exploit.
